troparion.text: O Creator of the universe, / Thou didst appoint times by Thy power; / bless the crown of this year with Thy goodness, O Lord. / Preserve in safety Thy rulers and cities: /  and through the intercessions of the Theotokos, save us!

kontakion.text: O Creator and Master of time and the ages, / Triune and Merciful God of all: / grant blessings for the course of this year, / and in Thy boundless mercy save those who worship Thee and cry out in fear: / “O Savior, grant blessings to all mankind!”

troparion.text: You were a pillar of patient endurance, / having imitated the forefathers, O Venerable One: / Job in suffering, and Joseph in temptations. / You lived like the bodiless ones while yet in the flesh, O Simeon, our Father. / Beseech Christ God that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text: Seeking the things of the Highest, / and having made your pillar a fiery chariot, you were joined to the heights. / Therefore, you have become a companion to the angels, O Venerable One, / and with them you are praying incessantly to Christ God for us all.

saints.name: Venerable Martha, mother of Saint Simeon Stylites

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile,  /  and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance.  /  By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe!  /  Our Mother Martha, pray to Christ God to save our souls!

Your holy martyr Aithalas, O Lord,  /  through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God.  /  For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries,  /  and shattered the powerless boldness of demons.  /  Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Aithalas;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

saints.name: Holy Forty Women Martyrs and Martyr Ammon the Deacon, their teacher, at Heraclea in Thrace

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs, O Lord,  /  through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God.  /  For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries,  /  and shattered the powerless boldness of demons.  /  Through their intercessions, save our souls!

saints.name: Martyrs Callista and her brothers, Evodius and Hermogenes, at Nicomedia

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs, O Lord,  /  through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God.  /  For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries,  /  and shattered the powerless boldness of demons.  /  Through their intercessions, save our souls!

saints.name: “Chernigov-Gethsemane” Icon of the Mother of God

troparion.text: Most Pure Sovereign Lady Theotokos, the hope of all Christians,  / since we have no other hope but you,  / Most Pure Sovereign Lady Theotokos and Mother of Christ my God;  / therefore have mercy and deliver me from all my afflictions,  / and entreat your merciful Son and God, /  that He may have mercy on my unfortunate soul and deliver me from eternal torment,  / and vouchsafe me His Heavenly Kingdom.

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile,  /  and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance.  /  By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe!  /  Our Father Meletius, pray to Christ God to save our souls!
